title: Cross-Linking and Strong Cation Exchange (SCX) Fractionation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cb5u8","text":"This is a method for performing Lys-Lys cross-linking on a purified organelle sample, in this case EEA1-positive early endosomes. The crosslinker used is DSSO ((Bis(2,5-dioxopyrrolidin-1-yl)\n3,3′-sulfinyldipropionate)). This cross-linker has a mass spec cleavable sulfoxide, which facilitates identification of individual cross-linked peptides.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8cktn","text":"disuccinimidyl sulfoxide (DSSO; Thermo Scientific, A33545)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8a6od","text":"2-chloroacetamide (Sigma-Aldrich, C0267) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f620b","text":"trypsin (Promega, V511C) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c021t","text":"Lys-C (Wako Chemicals, 129-02541)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dihnb","text":"Sep-Pak C8 50mg Cartridge (Waters, WAT054965)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cl5vf","text":"EPPS (3-[4-(2-Hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazine]propanesulfonic acid)  (Thermo Scientific, J61296AE)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ca6js","text":"dithiothreitol (DTT) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ab0r","text":"Empore SPE Disks C18 to generate stage tips(Sigma Millipore, 66883-U)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9putn","text":"PolyLC PolySulfoethyl A column (3 μm particle size, 2.1 mm inner diameter, and 100 mm length) (#102SE03--)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fgf3h","text":"Acetonitrile","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3peeb","text":"Formic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rle8","text":"Lys-C (Wako Chemicals, 129-02541)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Samples for cross-linking (e.g. individual organelles) are isolated using dedicated protocols such as https://doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ewov14pjyvr2/v2 for isolation of EEA1-positive early endosomes
Resuspend freshly prepared purified organelle pellets in KPBS (25 mM KCl, 100 mM potassium phosphate, 150 mM NaCl, pH 7.2) to a protein concentration of ~1μg/uL.
Cross-link the proteins by adding 1 mM DSSO (Bis(2,5-dioxopyrrolidin-1-yl) 3,3′-sulfinyldipropionate) and incubate at room temperature for 40 minutes.
Quench the cross-linking reaction by adding 50 mM Tris buffer (pH 7.5) and incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Denature the cross-linked samples by adding 8 M urea.
Reduce the proteins by adding 5 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) and incubate for 30 minutes at 37°C.
Alkylate the reduced proteins with 40 mM chloroacetamide for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Digest the cross-linked proteins with Lys-C enzyme at a ratio of 1:75 (enzyme) and incubate at 37°C overnight.
Dilute the urea concentration of the sample to 2 M using 50 mM EPPS buffer (pH 8.0).
Further digest the proteins by adding trypsin at a ratio of 1:100 (enzyme) and incubate at 37°C for 6 hours.
Desalt the peptides using Sep-Pak solid-phase extraction column.
Dry the desalted peptides in a SpeedVac concentrator. Time will depend on the vacuum strength.

Fractionate the dried peptides by strong cation exchange (SCX) chromatography. A 70-minute linear gradient at flow rate 0.18 mL/min was used as follows: 0% to 8% of mobile phase (0.5 M NaCl in 20% ACN, 0.05% formic acid) over 14 minutes. Increase to 20% at 28 minutes. Increase to 40% at 48 minutes. Increase to 90% at 68 minutes.
Collect fractions every 30 seconds starting at 35 minutes for 10 minutes, and then every minute thereafter.
Dry the collected fractions in a SpeedVac concentrator.
Desalt the dried fractions using StageTip.
Reconstitute fractions in 5% acetonitrile (ACN) and 5% formic acid.
Analyze the reconstituted fractions by liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS).

Alternatively, other cross-linker reagents could be use. For DHSO/DMTMM, 8 mM DHSO (3,3'-sulfinyldi(propanehydrazide)) and 16mM DMTMM (4-(4,6-dimethoxy-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)-4-methylmorpholinium chloride) at 37°C for 90 min. Proceed directly with protein precipitation. Samples can be digested using S-trap sample preparation following mini-spin column digestion protocol as provided by the manufacturer (Protifi, C02-mini-80).
creator: Harper JW
